prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier `standard`, PySCF). The calculation was performed in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1 for the neutral molecule. The N-1 (cation) state had a charge of +1 and a multiplicity of 2, and the N+1 (anion) state had a charge of -1 and a multiplicity of 2. The geometry was sourced from the user-provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz` file, and no geometry optimization was performed. The SCF for all states converged successfully.

The most electrophilic site was found to be Carbon 4 (atom index 3) with an f+ value of 0.49642. The most nucleophilic site was Carbon 5 (atom index 4) with an f- value of 0.10729.

A plot of the Fukui indices per atom is available at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_gemini-2.5-flash/20260704-185546_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
